body: PT Petrosea Tbk (PTRO) signed a mining service agreement with PT Niaga Jasa Dunia and PT Bara Prima Mandiri, with an estimated contract value of IDR 4.03 trillion, to provide overburden removal and coal excavation services for approximately 8 months. As of September 2024, PTRO reported a 21.75% year-over-year revenue increase to USD 418.8 million, but a 70.72% year-over-year net profit decline due to increased expenses.
title: Petrosea signs mining service agreement with PT Bara Prima Mandiri for coal excavation project in Central Kalimantan.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) closed at 7,383.87 on Wednesday, declining throughout the day due to slippages in blue-chip stocks, despite brief spikes. Meanwhile, some indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESGL showed a contrasting trend, with gains of up to 0.86%, while certain stocks like PT Amman Mineral Internasional tbk (AMMAN) and PT Mayora Indah tbk (MAYO) made strides.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Closes Lower Amid Slippages in Blue-Chip Stocks

body: PT Fap Agri Tbk (FAPA) plans to conduct another share buyback of up to 292.2 million shares, equivalent to 8.05% of its total issued and paid-up capital, to maintain its stock price at an optimum level and grant more flexibility in managing capital for its business expansion plan. The buyback, which will be implemented gradually within 12 months after approval, will utilize the company's internal cash, with the execution price to be determined in accordance with Article 10 and 11 of POJK 29/2023.
title: PT Fap Agri to Conduct Another Share Buyback of Up to 292.2 Million Shares After Securing Shareholder Approval.

body: PT Impack Pratama Tbk (IMPC) has injected IDR 79.9 billion of capital into its subsidiary, PT Sirkular Karya Indonesia (SKI), through the issuance of 80 million new shares, with IMPC taking over 79.99 million shares and Phillip Tjipto acquiring the remaining shares. As of September 30, 2024, SKI's total assets stood at IDR 29.51 billion, with IMPC holding a 99.9% stake in the company.
title: Impack Pratama injects IDR 79.9 billion into subsidiary PT Sirkular Karya Indonesia through new share issuance.

body: On November 5, foreign investors in Jakarta's stock market purchased a net 334.02 million shares, with top buys including PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with 76.56 million shares, PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) with 266.28 million shares, and PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk (BULL) with 103.73 million shares. Meanwhile, top sells included PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) with -190.48 million shares, PT MNC Land Tbk (KPIG) with -58.68 million shares, and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with -34.31 million shares.
title: Foreign investors in Jakarta's stock market purchase majority shares in digital and mining companies on Tuesday.

body: PT Makna Prakarsa Utama Tbk (MPUT) acquired 730 million shares of PT Mitra Tirta Buwana, gaining 73.21% control, while other notable transactions included PT Bogamulia Nagadi acquiring 3.22 million shares of PT Tempo Scan Pacific Tbk and PT Startel Communication buying 800,000 shares of PT Jaya Trishindo Tbk. Meanwhile, PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama sold 45.10 million shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk, and foreign investors such as DBS Bank Ltd and Inv Management Pte Ltd reduced their holdings in PT Lippo Karawaci Tbk and PT ITSec Asia Tbk, respectively.
title: Multiple companies in Indonesia see changes in share ownership and control following recent transactions.

body: Sabana Prawirawidjaja increased his stake in PT Ultrajaya Milk Industry & Trading Company Tbk (ULTJ) to nearly 50% by purchasing 1.6 billion shares, while Welly Thomas boosted his share portion in PT Sumber Global Energy Tbk (SGER) to 8.25% by buying 235.52 million shares. Meanwhile, other investors made notable transactions, including Cascade Gold Limited becoming a new investor in PT Pabrik Kertas Tjiwi Kimia Tbk, and foreign investors such as Inv Management Pte Ltd and IMR Asia Holding Pte Ltd selling shares in various companies.
title: Several investors and companies make notable transactions in the Indonesian stock market, including share purchases and sales.

body: Foreign investors generated a net foreign volume surplus of 192.04 million shares, driven by purchases of PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) shares worth 1.92 billion shares and PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) shares worth 454.11 million shares. Meanwhile, foreign investors sold shares of PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) worth 113.25 million shares, PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (SCMA) shares worth 40 million shares, and PT Matahari Putra Prima Tbk (MPPA) shares worth 53.33 million shares, among others.
title: Foreign investors increase share purchasing activities in Indonesia, driven by buying spree in digital and mining companies.

body: PT Avia Avian TBK (AVIA) will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 11 per share, totaling IDR 672.6 billion, to its shareholders, following approval from the company's board of directors and commissioners. The company reported a net profit of IDR 1.16 trillion for the first nine months of 2024, with an unrestricted retained earnings balance of IDR 1.69 trillion and total equity of IDR 10.04 trillion as of September 30, 2024.
title: PT Avia Avian to Distribute IDR 11 per Share Interim Dividend to Shareholders

body: PT Daaz Bara Lestari Tbk (no ticker mentioned) has set its initial public offering (IPO) price at IDR 900 per share, aiming to raise IDR 270 billion from the sale of 300 million shares, equivalent to 15.02% of its total issued and paid-up capital post-IPO. The company plans to utilize approximately 33.34% of the IPO proceeds to obtain nickel ores, with the remaining portion allocated to loan subsidiaries.
title: Daaz Bara Lestari sets IPO price at IDR 900 per share, aiming to raise IDR 270 billion.
